subject: Anatomy
definition: The skull is the bony skeleton of the head containing 22 bones (8 cranial and 14 facial).
It protects the brain and sensory structures, provides attachment for muscle mechanics, and houses the pathways of CRITICAL cranial nerves.
principle: Cranial bones articulation via fibrous sutures (coronal, sagittal, lambdoid).
The cranial cavity includes the anterior, middle, and posterior cranial fossae, each carrying vital blood structures and nerve roots.
equipment: Netter's anatomy atlas with transverse section profiles

parameters.Cranial Bones Count: 8 bones (Frontal, Parietal (2), Temporal (2), Occipital, Sphenoid, Ethmoid)
parameters.Facial Bones Count: 14 bones (Nasal, Lacrimal, Palatine, Inferior nasal concha, Vomer, Maxilla, Zygomatic, Mandible)
parameters.Critical Sutures: Fibrous joint articulations that fuse with age

mechanism: Skeletal rigidity absorbs external kinetic impact.
Meningeal sheets and cerebrospinal fluid distribute pressure gradients uniformly.

indications: Study of traumatic brain injuries (TBI) and temporal bone fractures
Pathology of cranial nerve entrapment syndromes

general: Cranial palpation in acute basilar fractures

treatmentProcedure: Analyze the margins of the orbit and nasal aperture.
Trace the path of cranial nerve VII through the stylo-mastoid foramen.
Audit temporal bone attachments of masseter and temporalis muscle networks.

precautions: Avoid applying excessive compressive pressure to infant fontanelles during palpation drills.
clinicalPearls: The Pterion is the weakest point on the lateral skull wall, where temporal, frontal, parietal, and sphenoid bones meet. It overlies the middle meningeal artery; trauma here can trigger rapid epidural hematoma.
vivaQuestions: Name the bones meeting at the Pterion and the underlying vascular risk. | temporal, frontal, parietal, and sphenoid bones; middle meningeal artery risk.
Which cranial nerve traverses the cribriform plate of the ethmoid? | Olfactory nerve (CN I).

mcqs.question: Which bone forms the posterior floor of the cranial cavity?
mcqs.explanation: The occipital bone forms the base of the skull posteriorly and houses the posterior cranial fossa.

reference: Chaurasia B.D. Textbook of Anatomy - Vol 3 (Head, Neck & Brain)
